vitals.date_specific: 2026: October 24-25 (Oct 24 registration/expo, Oct 25 race day, 08:00 start)
vitals.field_size: 1,000-rider cap across all three courses combined (2025: 715 riders from 17 countries)
vitals.start_format: Staggered starts from Çeşme city center: 91km at 08:00, 67km/41km at 08:30
vitals.entry_fee: Tiered by registration window and course: SALCANO 91km 1,250-2,100 TL; TOYOTA HYBRID 67km 990-1,650 TL; CESME 41km 900-1,300 TL (2026 pricing, rises through 3 booking windows May-October)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 63. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 60.

course_description.character: A road gran fondo on the Çeşme Peninsula, Aegean coast, Turkey, offering three loop courses that all start and finish in Çeşme city center: the SALCANO long course (91km, 1,025m+), the TOYOTA HYBRID medium course (67km, 787m+), and the CESME short course (41km, 382m+), staggered starting at 08:00/08:30. The event is produced by Argeus Travel & Events with VeloTurk, a Turkish cycling group tied to the 'Bir Çocuk Gülerse Dünya Güler' (If a Child Smiles, the World Smiles) bicycle-donation project; Salcano has been a title sponsor in recent editions (the 2025 edition was branded 'Veloturk Gran Fondo Çeşme by Salcano'). The race weekend runs Saturday registration/expo into a Sunday race day.

history.origin_story: Launched in 2016 in Çeşme, Izmir Province, produced by Argeus Travel & Events in cooperation with VeloTurk, a Turkish cycling group tied to a bicycle-donation social project. Çeşme Municipality called the 2025 running its ninth edition (the official history's own edition list skips 2020); the current site's 'eighth time' homepage copy is stale template text and doesn't match that dated edition history.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Real, Long-Running Aegean Fondo — But the Date on File Was Wrong By Six Months
biased_opinion.summary: Veloturk Gran Fondo Çeşme is a genuine, roughly decade-old road gran fondo on Turkey's Çeşme Peninsula, produced by Argeus Travel & Events with VeloTurk (a Turkish cycling group tied to a bicycle-donation social project) and listed on the Turkish Cycling Federation's official gran fondo calendar. The prior profile had the event scheduled for an unspecified April date — both the organizer's site and the federation calendar place the 2026 edition on October 25, six months off. The event itself offers three loop courses (91/67/41km) all starting and finishing in Çeşme city center, capped at 1,000 riders combined; 2025 drew 715 riders from 17 countries. The long course's 91km/1,025m profile is a moderate ride by this database's standards, not a marquee climbing test.
strengths: Genuinely long-running for the region — roughly a decade of editions since 2016 (ninth edition in 2025, though the official history skips 2020) with a stable organizer and venue
Independently confirmed on the Turkish Cycling Federation's official gran fondo calendar, not just the organizer's own marketing
Real, published logistics: a firm 1,000-rider cap across three distances, tiered registration pricing, and verified 2025 turnout (715 riders, 17 countries) — this isn't a paper event
Charity/community tie-in through VeloTurk's bicycle-donation project gives the event an identity beyond a standard commercial sportive
weaknesses: The date on file was wrong by roughly six months (April vs. the actual October 25 date) — a real data-quality failure for anyone trying to plan around it
The long course's 91km/1,025m profile is modest — climbing and distance are well below what most Tier 1-2 European gran fondos in this database offer, despite sharing the tier bracket here

No independent (non-organizer, non-municipal) media coverage found beyond local Turkish press and the Cycling Federation calendar listing — hard to gauge rider-reported course quality or organization independent of promotional sources
Registration fees escalate through three booking windows (roughly 70% higher for late sign-up on the long course) — a real cost penalty for riders who don't commit early
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A real, long-running regional fondo with genuine logistics (rider cap, tiered pricing, verified turnout) behind it — but go in expecting a moderate single-day course rather than a major European-style climbing test, and double-check the date directly with the organizer given this database's prior six-month error.

final_verdict.one_liner: Established Aegean-coast gran fondo on the Çeşme Peninsula — a modest 91km/1,025m single loop, not a major climbing test.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Worth considering if you're already in the Izmir/Çeşme region and want a well-established local fondo with a charity angle. Not worth traveling far for on the strength of the course alone — the distance and climbing are modest.
final_verdict.alternatives: Other Turkish Cycling Federation gran fondo calendar events (e.g. Bodrum, AKRA Gran Fondo) for a similar regional Turkish fondo circuit experience.

eligibility.notes: CORRECTIONS (2026-07-24): date changed from '2026: April TBD' to '2026: October 25' — both the official 2026 course page and the Turkish Cycling Federation's 2026 gran fondo calendar PDF confirm October 25, 2026 (weekend of Oct 24-25); the April date on file was wrong and doesn't match any Çeşme entry on the federation calendar (April slots belong to other races like Bodrum/AKRA). distance_km changed from 91.7 to 91, elevation_m from 1036 to 1025, matching the official long/SALCANO course page's finish table (live-curl-verified). founded changed from 2017 to 2016 per the official event history and Çeşme Municipality's 2025 ninth-edition announcement (that history skips 2020). Organizer identified as Argeus Travel & Events with VeloTurk (a Turkish cycling group tied to a bicycle-donation social project) — 'VeloTurk' is part of the event's real brand name, not a misattribution. Added route_options (91/67/41km courses with official elevation figures), field_size (1,000-rider cap; 2025: 715 riders/17 countries per Çeşme Municipality), entry_fee (tiered by course and registration window) via live curl of the official course page. Confirmed active: 2025 edition held Oct 26, 2025; 2026 program/registration live on official site; listed on the federation's March 2026 calendar update. SOL REVIEW: rejected an initial 'no independently reported field-size/entry-fee data' weakness claim after live-curling the official page and finding published fees + rider cap; corrected 'continuous editions' (2020 is missing from the official edition history) and softened an unsupported 'single-loop'/'sponsor branding unstable' framing.
